# Branch onto always uses rebase, even when merge method is configured.
# This is necessary to correctly transplant commits from one base to another:
# a merge cannot remove old-base history from the branch.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-05-27T18:24:42Z'

mkdir repo
cd repo
git init
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# Configure merge-based restacking
git config spice.restack.method merge

# Create two base branches
git add feat1.txt
gs bc feat1 -m feat1
gs trunk
git add feat2.txt
gs bc feat2 -m feat2

# Create a branch on top of feat1
gs branch checkout feat1
git add child.txt
gs bc child -m child

# Move child onto feat2.
# Even though the restack method is merge, branch onto uses rebase
# so that old-base (feat1) changes are not included in child.
gs branch onto feat2

# Verify no merge commit was created (linear history)
git log --oneline --graph child
! stdout 'Restack: merge'

# Verify child is now on feat2
gs log short
stderr 'child'
stderr 'feat2'

# Verify feat1 changes are NOT present in child
! exists feat1.txt
exists feat2.txt
exists child.txt

-- repo/init.txt --
initial
-- repo/feat1.txt --
feature 1
-- repo/feat2.txt --
feature 2
-- repo/child.txt --
child feature
